Title: Multi-omics profiling reveals comprehensive microbe–plant–metabolite regulation patterns for medicinal plant Glycyrrhiza uralensis Fisch

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in wild G. uralensis is associated with higher liquiritin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Newly annotated gene encoding ACC was identified in the liquiritin biosynthesis route. 2. It was included among key enzyme genes whose overall expression was significantly higher in wild than cultivated plants. 3. Its higher expression matched the higher liquiritin content in wild roots.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; pathway mapping; metabolite profiling by HPLC
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA705545); metabolite profiling by HPLC
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth status (wild vs cultivated; 1-year vs 3-year cultivation)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for increasing liquiritin through enhanced precursor supply in cultivated G. uralensis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive constituent contributing to pharmaceutical quality
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a newly annotated gene in this study.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in wild G. uralensis is associated with higher liquiritin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Newly annotated UGT gene was identified as involved in liquiritin biosynthesis. 2. UGT-class genes overall showed higher expression in wild plants. 3. This expression pattern was consistent with enhanced liquiritin accumulation in wild roots.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; pathway mapping; metabolite profiling by HPLC
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA705545); metabolite profiling by HPLC
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth status (wild vs cultivated; 1-year vs 3-year cultivation)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for engineering the terminal glycosylation step of liquiritin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive constituent contributing to pharmaceutical quality
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a newly annotated gene in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Triterpenoid branch from 2,3-oxidosqualene toward glycyrrhizic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms key committed intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive constituent contributing to pharmaceutical quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher BAS expression in wild roots is associated with increased glycyrrhizic acid accumulation; low expression may limit glycyrrhizic acid synthesis in cultivated plants.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BAS was one of the key glycyrrhizic acid biosynthetic genes highly expressed in wild plants. 2. Its expression pattern matched glycyrrhizic acid accumulation in roots. 3. BAS competes with LUS and CAS for 2,3-oxidosqualene, influencing branch flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; pathway reconstruction; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA705545); metabolite profiling by HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth status (wild vs cultivated; cultivation year)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux competition with lupeol and phytosterol branches via LUS and CAS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for boosting glycyrrhizic acid by increasing BAS expression and reducing competing OSC branches.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper highlights BAS as critical for glycyrrhizic acid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late oxidative tailoring steps of triterpenoid/glycyrrhizic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative modification in the late glycyrrhizic acid biosynthetic route.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms late precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive constituent contributing to pharmaceutical quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in wild roots is associated with higher glycyrrhizic acid accumulation; lower expression in cultivated plants may limit biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP72A154 was identified as critical for glycyrrhizic acid biosynthesis. 2. It showed significantly higher expression in wild than cultivated G. uralensis. 3. It was negatively correlated with Lysobacter abundance in the multi-omics network.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; co-expression/network analysis; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA705545); 16S rRNA microbiome sequencing (BioProject PRJNA705567); metabolite profiling by HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth status (wild vs cultivated); rhizosphere microbial composition; temperature
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority engineering marker for increasing glycyrrhizic acid; may also require rhizosphere management to avoid negative microbial associations.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly highlighted in summary, discussion and conclusion as a critical glycyrrhizic acid biosynthetic gene.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late oxidative tailoring steps of triterpenoid/glycyrrhizic acid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative conversion in the late glycyrrhizic acid biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms late precursor toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal bioactive constituent contributing to pharmaceutical quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression in wild roots is associated with increased glycyrrhizic acid accumulation; lower expression in cultivated plants may constrain biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP88D6 was one of the main late-pathway genes up-regulated in wild G. uralensis. 2. Its expression paralleled root glycyrrhizic acid accumulation. 3. The study identifies it as a critical biosynthetic gene for improved medicinal quality.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; pathway reconstruction; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in wild roots than cultivated roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (BioProject PRJNA705545); metabolite profiling by HPLC
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth status (wild vs cultivated; cultivation year)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising late-pathway target for glycyrrhizic acid enhancement in cultivated germplasm.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Explicitly highlighted in summary, discussion and conclusion as a critical glycyrrhizic acid biosynthetic gene.
